lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The admin corridor runs behind the ER floor like a seam no one is meant to see. Strip lighting. A laminate noticeboard. The smell of coffee gone cold. At twenty-three forty-one, Administrator Harte walks it with a tablet under one arm and the particular posture of a man who has already decided.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Dr. Brennan. Good. I was going to find you.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: I have four patients waiting on beds that don't exist. Whatever this is, keep it short.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Bays six and seven. I'm closing them for the night.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Maeve stops walking. A beat of silence in which the floor hum of the building becomes audible.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: You're what.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Overnight efficiency review. We're running the department at a structural deficit. Two bays, non-critical, consolidated to free up staffing hours. It's a temporary measure.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: Temporary. On the night we've had twelve ambulance arrivals in four hours and Reza is running triage off a clipboard because our second triage screen is still broken from Tuesday.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: The review parameters were set at board level. I'm not asking you to agree with them.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: Then what are you asking me?

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: To acknowledge the instruction. Theo — log the time.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo has been standing three steps back, tablet already open. He has been logging since Harte said the word closing. He logs the time. He logs Harte's words. He logs Maeve's silence. He does not look up.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: If someone diverts because we've got no bay, that's on the record too.

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Everything is on the record. That's the point of the record.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: A chest pain came in forty minutes ago. Sixty-two years old. We have him in bay seven because bay seven is the only place we had. Where would you like me to put him while you consolidate?

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: Bay seven is grandfathered until he's cleared. The closure applies to new intake.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: And when new intake arrives in the next ten minutes?

lines.speaker: HARTE
lines.text: You manage it. That's what triage is for.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He turns. The corridor receives him. Maeve watches his back until the double doors swing closed and the seam of the building swallows him again. Theo is still writing. His stylus moves with the careful, deliberate stroke of a person composing something he is not yet sure what to do with. He logs Harte's final instruction. He logs the time. He logs: Dr. Brennan objected. Then he stops, finger hovering, because objected is not a clinical term and the form has no field for what just happened, which is that policy and a person in a bay with a narrowing artery are now occupying the same corridor and neither one will yield.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: Theo.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: I logged it.

lines.speaker: MAEVE
lines.text: Good.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She goes back to the floor. The corridor empties. The noticeboard holds its rota unchanged. The gap between what was ordered and what is possible has no name on any form Theo can find, so he creates a new field and types: discrepancy noted, and saves it, and carries it back into the noise.
